New OpenAI codeex updates are insane. OpenAI just dropped something that is going to change how you code forever. Today, I'm going to show you the new OpenAI codeex updates. And honestly, this is the craziest thing I've seen in AI coding all year. We're talking AI agents that work for you while you sleep. No joke. This is going to blow your mind. So, stick around because you do not want to miss this. So, OpenAI just quietly updated Codeex and most people have no idea which is wild because this is a big deal, like a really big deal. Let me break it down for you real quick. Open AAI codeex is basically an AI coding command center. Think of it like this. You know how you normally talk to an AI, ask it to write some code, copy paste it, test it, fix it, go back and forth. That whole thing is slow. Codeex throws all of that out the window. With codeex, you can run multiple AI coding agents at the same time. Not one, multiple. Each one works on its own copy of your code. So they don't step on each other. They don't break things. They just work in parallel while you sit back and watch. Each agent gets its own little workspace called a work tree. It's like giving each AI its own desk. They write code. They run tests. They submit changes. And you just review what they did. That alone is already insane. But what OpenAI just added makes it even crazier. And I'm going to show you exactly what they did. But first, here's what most people get wrong about AI coding tools. They think it's just autocomplete, like it just finishes your sentences. No, Codeex is way beyond that. This is a full system that manages agents, runs workflows, and handles your whole project. Keep that in your head because what I'm about to show you next, builds on top of all of that. So, update number one, Codeex themes. Now, I know what you're thinking. Themes? That's the big update. Hold up, let me explain why this actually matters. You can now fully customize the entire codeex interface. We're talking base theme, accent colors, background colors, foreground colors, UI fonts, code fonts, everything. You can make it look however you want. And here's the cool part. You can share your themes with other developers or import themes that other people made. Why does this matter? Because developers live inside their tools. If you've ever used VS Code, you know people spend hours picking the right dark theme, the right syntax colors, the right font. It's not just about looks. It's about comfort. It's about being able to stare at code for hours without your eyes falling out. And now Codeex lets you do that. It turns Codeex from a tool you visit into a place you actually want to work in. Small change, big impact, trust me. But that's not even the main event. The real update is what I'm about to tell you next. And this one is a game changer. Hey, if we haven't met already, I'm the digital avatar of Julian Goldie, CEO of Goldie Agency. Whilst he's helping clients get more leads and customers, I'm here to help you get the latest AI updates. Julian Goldie reads every comment, so make sure you comment below. All right, so update number two, and this is the one that made my jaw drop. Codeex automations are now fully released. General availability, that means everyone can use them now. And let me tell you what this means in plain English. You can now set up AI agents that run coding tasks automatically on a schedule in the background without you doing anything. Let me say that again. AI agents running code tasks while you're not even at your computer, while you're eating, while you're sleeping, while you're at the gym, they just run. Think about that for a second. You set it up once and it keeps going every day, every hour, however you set it. That is not a coding assistant. That is a coding coworker. Let me give you some real examples so you can see how powerful this is. Example one, daily repo summary. You tell codeex every morning look at my code, summarize the key commits, open issues, and pull request changes. And every single morning it does it automatically. You wake up, the summary is there. Done. Example two, bug triage. You tell Codeex, review all new issues on my project and label them as bug feature request or question. And it does it every time a new issue comes in. No manual sorting, no wasted time. Example three, pull request reviews. You tell Codeex, "Review all open pull requests and suggest improvements or flags security issues." And it goes through every single one, gives you feedback, points out problems before you even look at it yourself. Example four, code cleanup. You tell Codeex, "Find unused imports and refactor outdated code." And it just tidies things up, like having a cleaning crew for your codebase. Now, here's where it gets really interesting, and I haven't even told you about the controls yet. Now, if you want to learn how to use AI tools like OpenAI Codeex to automate your workflows and scale your business, you need to check out the AI Profit Boardroom. It's the best place to scale your business, get more customers, and save hundreds of hours with AI automation. I'll drop the link in the comments and description. Seriously, if you're into this stuff, that community will change everything for you. Go check it out. All right, back to the good stuff. So, with the new automation controls, you get to pick exactly how the automation runs. And there are four big things you can set. First, you choose the model. You can pick which AI model runs your automation. Want the most powerful one? Use it. Want a faster, lighter one for simple tasks? Use that instead. You're in control. Second, you set the reasoning level. This is cool. You can tell the AI how hard to think. Fast reasoning for quick stuff, balanced for normal tasks. Deep reasoning for complex problems. You decide how much brain power it uses. Third, you choose the environment. You can run the automation in a work tree, which is the safest option, is completely isolated, can't break anything, or you can run it directly on an existing branch if you want it to make changes right there. Your call. Fourth, workflow templates. This is huge. Instead of setting up the same automation over and over, you can save templates. Daily repo report template, issue triage template, PR review template, set it once, reuse it forever. That saves so much time. Now, let me explain how all of this works together because the mental model is actually really simple. There are three steps. Thread, workree, review. The thread is your conversation with the AI agent. You tell it what to do. The work tree is the isolated copy of your code where it does the work. And the review cue is where the results show up for you to check and approve. That's it. Simple, clean, safe. The AI does the work in its own space. You review it when you're ready. Nothing gets pushed to your main code unless you say so. And that's what makes this so powerful. It's not just fast, it's safe. You're not handing your entire project to an AI and hoping for the best. You're giving it tasks in a controlled environment and reviewing everything before it goes live. So, let me tell you what this really means for the future. Codeex just went from being an AI coding assistant to being an autonomous coding co-orker. It runs tasks without you prompting it. It works while you're offline. It executes workflows daily or hourly. That is not a tool. That is a team member. and we're just getting started. Think about where this goes in 6 months, in a year. AI agents that manage entire code bases that fix bugs before you even know they exist, that write documentation, that review every single pull request, that keep your project clean and organized every single day without you lifting a finger. This is the future of software development and it's happening right now, not next year, not someday, right now. So, if you're a developer, you need to be paying attention to this. If you're building a product, you need to be looking at how codecs can speed up your workflow.
